summary: The share of single adults who return to shelter within a year of a permanent-housing exit has fallen fairly steadily since FY22, which DHS attributes to the city's expanded investment in subsidized rental assistance (including CityFHEPS) generating more stable, lasting placements. The small uptick in the FY26 year-to-date figure reflects a rise specifically in returns among people who left shelter without a subsidy, partly offset by continued low return rates for subsidized exits.

sources.label: DHS, Preliminary Mayor's Management Report, Fiscal 2026
